revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Kōzaki-jinja (Kōzaki, Chiba)]][[ja:神崎神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kōzaki-jinja (Kōzaki, Chiba)]][[ja:神崎神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kōzaki-jinja (Kōzaki, Chiba)]][[ja:神崎神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Kozaki-shrine and NanjyaMonjya,Kozaki-town,Japan.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Kōzaki|WD=Q1153721}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.905556|N|140.4|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}}}} {{ill|Kōzaki Shrine|ja|神崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzaki Shrine|qq=}} {{Otheruses|Kōzaki Shrine in Chiba Prefecture|Shrine in Kotoura, Tottori Prefecture|Kōzaki Shrine (Kotoura)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |名称= Kōzaki Shrine |image=[[File:2024-kasai_Kozaki_Shrine_and_Kozaki_no_O-kusu_Katori-JPN.jpg|250px|{{ill|Kōzaki Shrine|ja|神崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzaki Shrine|qq=}}
Worship Hall and the Nanjamonja tree]] |所在地= {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|Katori District|en|Katori District|WD=Q780284|lt=Katori District}}{{ill|Kōzaki|en|Kōzaki|WD=Q1153721|lt=Kozaki Town}} Kozaki Honjuku 1994 |位置= {{coord|35|54|20|N|140|24|0|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja|天鳥船命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥之石楠船神|1=Tori no Iwakusufune no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10731425}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi no Mikoto}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona no Mikoto}} |社格= Prefectural shrine |創建= |本殿= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= Komatsu Shrine |例祭= |神事= }} '''Kōzaki Shrine''' (神崎神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|Katori District|en|Katori District|WD=Q780284|lt=Katori District}}{{ill|Kōzaki|en|Kōzaki|WD=Q1153721|lt=Kozaki Town}} ({{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province}}{{ill|Katori District|en|Katori District|WD=Q780284|lt=Katori District}}). Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} was defined under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Prefectural shrine|lt_ja=県社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It enshrines as its main deities {{ill|Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja|天鳥船命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥之石楠船神|1=Tori no Iwakusufune no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10731425}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona no Mikoto}}. == Overview == The founding date of this shrine is unknown, but according to its tradition, it is said that in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} era ({{ill|673|en|673|WD=Q31105|lt=673}}) it was relocated to this site from the two mounds at Ōura Pond, located on the border between {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|WD=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi}} and Shimōsa. Until the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} period, it was known as "Komatsu Shrine", but in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Genkei}} era ({{ill|879|en|879|WD=Q25001|lt=879}}) it was conferred the lower Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}下) in the {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|en|Shinkai (divine rank)|WD=Q11591025|lt=Shinto Ranking system}}.In the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', in the entry for April 5 in the 3rd year of the Genkei era, it is recorded: "To the {{ill|Mihosu Shrine|ja|御穂神社|lt=Miryōjin|lt_ja=御廊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosu Shrine|1=Miho Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489249}} of Suruga Province with the upper Junior Fifth Rank, the lower Senior Fifth Rank is conferred, and to the Komatsu Shrine of Shimōsa Province with the upper Sixth Rank, the lower Junior Fifth Rank is conferred." This shrine is not an auxiliary shrine ({{ill|Auxiliary shrine|ja|末社|lt=Massha|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Auxiliary shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}}) of {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Shrine}}, but during the periodic rebuilding it played a regular role in constructing the "Asame Hall". Furthermore, according to the ''{{ill|Tone River Illustrated History|ja|利根川図志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tone River Illustrated History|1=Tonegawa Zushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17229342}}'', this shrine was once part of the former territory of a chief priestly family, and its enshrined deity, {{ill|Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja|天鳥船命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥之石楠船神|1=Tori no Iwakusufune no Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10731425}}, is regarded as the messenger dispatched under the command of {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} when {{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|lt=Keizutsushi no Kami}} of {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Shrine}} and {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi no Kami}} of {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|lt=Kashima Shrine}} negotiated the {{ill|Land Concession|ja|葦原中国平定|lt=transfer of sovereignty|lt_ja=国譲り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Land Concession|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国譲り|1=Kuni-yuzuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621119}} with Ōkuninushi. It is also noted that the Tennichibune, a ship upon which deities ride ({{ill|Ship|ja|諸手船|lt=Various-hand ship|lt_ja=船|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ship|1=Ship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632096}}), is alternatively known as Tori no Ishikusu-fune no Kami, while this shrine possesses a large {{ill|Camphora officinarum|en|Camphora officinarum|WD=Q158722|lt=Kusunoki}}, commonly called the {{ill|Nanjamonja|ja|ナンジャモンジャ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanjamonja|1=Nanjamonja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324098}} tree.{{ill|Mito Kōmon|ja|水戸黄門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mito Kōmon|1=stories of Mito Kōmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11129919}} is said to have once pondered, "What on earth is the name of this tree?" Although he did not actually travel throughout Japan, he is known to have visited this shrine. The main tree is surrounded by five saplings; its trunk has a circumference of 13.1 meters and a height of 19 meters, and it is designated as a national {{ill|Natural monument|en|Natural monument|WD=Q23790|lt=Natural Monument}} under the name {{ill|Great Kozaki Kusunoki|ja|神崎の大クス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kozaki Kusunoki|1=Kozaki no Okusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124285355}}. The shrine grove, known as the "Kozaki Forest", is designated as a natural monument of Chiba Prefecture ({{ill|Natural Monuments of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#天然記念物|lt=Chiba Prefecture Natural Monuments|lt_ja=千葉県の天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural Monuments of Chiba Prefecture|1=Natural Monuments of Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}). [[File:Kōzaki-jinja_(Kōzaki,_Chiba)_12.jpg|thumb|right|230px|{{ill|Great Kozaki Kusunoki|ja|神崎の大クス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kozaki Kusunoki|1=Kozaki no Okusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124285355}}. Commonly known as the Nanjamonja tree.]] The shrine has long received strong patronage from successive rulers, and it is said that during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|Sengoku period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} it possessed shrine domains covering 700 {{ill|Town (unit)|ja|町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Town (unit)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=town of Japan|WD=Q1059478}} ({{ill|Jisha-ryō|en|Jisha-ryō|WD=Q11458058|lt=Shrine domain}}). During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it held 20 koku of {{ill|Shuin land|ja|朱印地・黒印地|lt=Red Seal Land|lt_ja=朱印領|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin land|1=shuinchi and kokuinchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521520}}, and the "Kōzaki Shrine Documents"—a collection of ancient manuscripts and illustrations passed down from the early {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}}—is considered an extremely important historical source of the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|WD=Q12554|lt=Medieval}} period ({{ill|Historical source|en|Historical source|WD=Q3750478|lt=Historical source}}), having been designated as a tangible cultural property (ancient documents) of Chiba Prefecture ({{ill|Tangible Cultural Property (Ancient Documents) of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#古文書|lt=Chiba Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property (Ancient Documents)|lt_ja=千葉県の有形文化財（古文書）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tangible Cultural Property (Ancient Documents) of Chiba Prefecture|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Ancient Documents) of Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}). Furthermore, the "Jinguji Documents" held by the former head temple ({{ill|Head temple|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=bettō-ji|WD=Q11396755}}) {{ill|Jinguji|ja|神宮寺 (千葉県神崎町)|lt=Jinguji|lt_ja=神宮寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinguji|1=Jingū-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589479}}, despite missing 60 out of 600 volumes of the {{ill|Large Prajñāpāramitā Sūtras|en|Large Prajñāpāramitā Sūtras|WD=Q15905785|lt=Mahaprajnaparamita Sutra}}, are highly valued as a sutra transcription from the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period}}. The colophon ({{ill|Colophon|ja|奥書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Colophon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥付|1=colophon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q372474}}) reveals details about the temples ({{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|WD=Q5393308|lt=Temple}}), monks ({{ill|Monk|en|Monk|WD=Q733786|lt=Monk}}), and the sphere of religious influence at that time, and it has also been designated as a tangible cultural property of Chiba Prefecture ({{ill|Tangible Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#古文書|lt=Chiba Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property|lt_ja=千葉県の有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tangible Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|1=Tangible Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}). == Transportation == * About a 20‐minute walk from {{ill|Narita Line|en|Narita Line|WD=Q1070027|lt=Narita Line}}{{ill|Shimōsa-Kōzaki Station|en|Shimōsa-Kōzaki Station|WD=Q7497064|lt=Shimōsa-Kozaki Station}} * Near the south end of Kozaki Ōhashi on {{ill|Japan National Route 356|en|Japan National Route 356|WD=Q6157325|lt=National Route 356}} and {{ill|Ibaraki and Chiba Prefectural Road 107, Edosaki-Kozaki Line|ja|茨城県道・千葉県道107号江戸崎神崎線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki and Chiba Prefectural Road 107, Edosaki-Kozaki Line|1=Ibaraki Prefectural Road and Chiba Prefectural Road Route 107|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617789}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Kenichi Tanikawa|ja|谷川健一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenichi Tanikawa|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2392596}} ''The Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Sites – Volume 11: Kanto'' {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusuisha|1=Hakusuisha Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346470}}, 1984. ISBN 4-560-02221-6 == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Kozaki Manor|ja|神崎荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kozaki Manor|1=Kozaki Manor|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121862999}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kozaki.chiba.jp/05kanko/history.html Kozaki Town History and Cultural Properties] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/7528/ Chiba Tourism Complete Guide: Great Kozaki Kusunoki (Kozaki Town)] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|神崎神社|version=98849858|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:神崎神社]] {{Commons category|Kōzaki-jinja (Kōzaki, Chiba)}} {{wikidata link|Q11589654}} [[Category:Architecture of Kōzaki Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kōzaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Kōzaki-jinja (Kōzaki, Chiba)]][[ja:神崎神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kōzaki-jinja (Kōzaki, Chiba)]][[ja:神崎神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kōzaki-jinja (Kōzaki, Chiba)]][[ja:神崎神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Kozaki-shrine and NanjyaMonjya,Kozaki-town,Japan.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Kōzaki|WD=Q1153721|1=Kōzaki}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.905556|N|140.4|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}}} {{ill|Kōzaki Shrine|ja|神崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzaki Shrine|qq=}} {{Otheruses|Kōzaki Shrine in Chiba Prefecture|Shrine in Kotoura, Tottori Prefecture|Kōzaki Shrine (Kotoura)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |名称= Kōzaki Shrine |image=[[File:2024-kasai_Kozaki_Shrine_and_Kozaki_no_O-kusu_Katori-JPN.jpg|250px|{{ill|Kōzaki Shrine|ja|神崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzaki Shrine|qq=}}
Worship Hall and the Nanjamonja tree]] |所在地= {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|Katori District|en|Katori District|WD=Q780284|lt=Katori District|1=Katori District}}{{ill|Kōzaki|en|Kōzaki|WD=Q1153721|lt=Kozaki Town|1=Kōzaki}} Kozaki Honjuku 1994 |位置= {{coord|35|54|20|N|140|24|0|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja|天鳥船命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥之石楠船神|1=Ame-no-torifune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10731425}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi no Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona no Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} |社格= Prefectural shrine |創建= |本殿= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= Komatsu Shrine |例祭= |神事= }} '''Kōzaki Shrine''' (神崎神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|Katori District|en|Katori District|WD=Q780284|lt=Katori District|1=Katori District}}{{ill|Kōzaki|en|Kōzaki|WD=Q1153721|lt=Kozaki Town|1=Kōzaki}} ({{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province|1=Shimōsa Province}}{{ill|Katori District|en|Katori District|WD=Q780284|lt=Katori District|1=Katori District}}). Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}} was defined under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Prefectural shrine|lt_ja=県社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It enshrines as its main deities {{ill|Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja|天鳥船命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥之石楠船神|1=Ame-no-torifune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10731425}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi no Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}, and {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona no Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}. == Overview == The founding date of this shrine is unknown, but according to its tradition, it is said that in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} era ({{ill|673|en|673|WD=Q31105|lt=673|1=673}}) it was relocated to this site from the two mounds at Ōura Pond, located on the border between {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|WD=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi|1=Hitachi Province}} and Shimōsa. Until the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} period, it was known as "Komatsu Shrine", but in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Genkei|1=Gangyō}} era ({{ill|879|en|879|WD=Q25001|lt=879|1=879}}) it was conferred the lower Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}下) in the {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|en|Shinkai (divine rank)|WD=Q11591025|lt=Shinto Ranking system|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}.In the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', in the entry for April 5 in the 3rd year of the Genkei era, it is recorded: "To the {{ill|Mihosu Shrine|ja|御穂神社|lt=Miryōjin|lt_ja=御廊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosu Shrine|1=Miho Shrine (Q11489249)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489249}} of Suruga Province with the upper Junior Fifth Rank, the lower Senior Fifth Rank is conferred, and to the Komatsu Shrine of Shimōsa Province with the upper Sixth Rank, the lower Junior Fifth Rank is conferred." This shrine is not an auxiliary shrine ({{ill|Auxiliary shrine|ja|末社|lt=Massha|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Auxiliary shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}}) of {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Shrine|1=Katori Jingū}}, but during the periodic rebuilding it played a regular role in constructing the "Asame Hall". Furthermore, according to the ''{{ill|Tone River Illustrated History|ja|利根川図志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tone River Illustrated History|1=Tonegawa Zushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17229342}}'', this shrine was once part of the former territory of a chief priestly family, and its enshrined deity, {{ill|Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja|天鳥船命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥之石楠船神|1=Ame-no-torifune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10731425}}, is regarded as the messenger dispatched under the command of {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} when {{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|lt=Keizutsushi no Kami|1=Futsunushi}} of {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Shrine|1=Katori Jingū}} and {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi no Kami|1=Kashima Jingū}} of {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|lt=Kashima Shrine|1=Kashima Jingū}} negotiated the {{ill|Land Concession|ja|葦原中国平定|lt=transfer of sovereignty|lt_ja=国譲り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Land Concession|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国譲り|1=Kuni-yuzuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621119}} with Ōkuninushi. It is also noted that the Tennichibune, a ship upon which deities ride ({{ill|Ship|ja|諸手船|lt=Various-hand ship|lt_ja=船|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ship|1=Various-hand ship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632096}}), is alternatively known as Tori no Ishikusu-fune no Kami, while this shrine possesses a large {{ill|Camphora officinarum|en|Camphora officinarum|WD=Q158722|lt=Kusunoki|1=Camphora officinarum}}, commonly called the {{ill|Nanjamonja|ja|ナンジャモンジャ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanjamonja|1=Q11324098|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324098}} tree.{{ill|Mito Kōmon|ja|水戸黄門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mito Kōmon|1=stories of Mito Kōmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11129919}} is said to have once pondered, "What on earth is the name of this tree?" Although he did not actually travel throughout Japan, he is known to have visited this shrine. The main tree is surrounded by five saplings; its trunk has a circumference of 13.1 meters and a height of 19 meters, and it is designated as a national {{ill|Natural monument|en|Natural monument|WD=Q23790|lt=Natural Monument|1=Natural monument}} under the name {{ill|Great Kozaki Kusunoki|ja|神崎の大クス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kozaki Kusunoki|1=Kozaki no Okusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124285355}}. The shrine grove, known as the "Kozaki Forest", is designated as a natural monument of Chiba Prefecture ({{ill|Natural Monuments of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#天然記念物|lt=Chiba Prefecture Natural Monuments|lt_ja=千葉県の天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural Monuments of Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture Natural Monuments|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}). [[File:Kōzaki-jinja_(Kōzaki,_Chiba)_12.jpg|thumb|right|230px|{{ill|Great Kozaki Kusunoki|ja|神崎の大クス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kozaki Kusunoki|1=Kozaki no Okusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124285355}}. Commonly known as the Nanjamonja tree.]] The shrine has long received strong patronage from successive rulers, and it is said that during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|Sengoku period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} it possessed shrine domains covering 700 {{ill|Town (unit)|ja|町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Town (unit)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Towns of Japan|WD=Q1059478}} ({{ill|Jisha-ryō|en|Jisha-ryō|WD=Q11458058|lt=Shrine domain|1=Jisha-ryō}}). During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it held 20 koku of {{ill|Shuin land|ja|朱印地・黒印地|lt=Red Seal Land|lt_ja=朱印領|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin land|1=shuinchi and kokuinchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521520}}, and the "Kōzaki Shrine Documents"—a collection of ancient manuscripts and illustrations passed down from the early {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}—is considered an extremely important historical source of the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|WD=Q12554|lt=Medieval|1=Middle Ages}} period ({{ill|Historical source|en|Historical source|WD=Q3750478|lt=Historical source|1=Historical source}}), having been designated as a tangible cultural property (ancient documents) of Chiba Prefecture ({{ill|Tangible Cultural Property (Ancient Documents) of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#古文書|lt=Chiba Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property (Ancient Documents)|lt_ja=千葉県の有形文化財（古文書）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tangible Cultural Property (Ancient Documents) of Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property (Ancient Documents)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}). Furthermore, the "Jinguji Documents" held by the former head temple ({{ill|Head temple|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Bettō-ji|WD=Q11396755}}) {{ill|Jinguji|ja|神宮寺 (千葉県神崎町)|lt=Jinguji|lt_ja=神宮寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinguji|1=Jingū-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589479}}, despite missing 60 out of 600 volumes of the {{ill|Large Prajñāpāramitā Sūtras|en|Large Prajñāpāramitā Sūtras|WD=Q15905785|lt=Mahaprajnaparamita Sutra|1=Large Prajñāpāramitā Sūtras}}, are highly valued as a sutra transcription from the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}. The colophon ({{ill|Colophon|ja|奥書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Colophon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥付|1=Colophon (publishing)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q372474}}) reveals details about the temples ({{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|WD=Q5393308|lt=Temple|1=Buddhist temple}}), monks ({{ill|Monk|en|Monk|WD=Q733786|lt=Monk|1=Monk}}), and the sphere of religious influence at that time, and it has also been designated as a tangible cultural property of Chiba Prefecture ({{ill|Tangible Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#古文書|lt=Chiba Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property|lt_ja=千葉県の有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tangible Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}). == Transportation == * About a 20‐minute walk from {{ill|Narita Line|en|Narita Line|WD=Q1070027|lt=Narita Line|1=Narita Line}}{{ill|Shimōsa-Kōzaki Station|en|Shimōsa-Kōzaki Station|WD=Q7497064|lt=Shimōsa-Kozaki Station|1=Shimōsa-Kōzaki Station}} * Near the south end of Kozaki Ōhashi on {{ill|Japan National Route 356|en|Japan National Route 356|WD=Q6157325|lt=National Route 356|1=Japan National Route 356}} and {{ill|Ibaraki and Chiba Prefectural Road 107, Edosaki-Kozaki Line|ja|茨城県道・千葉県道107号江戸崎神崎線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki and Chiba Prefectural Road 107, Edosaki-Kozaki Line|1=Ibaraki Prefectural Road and Chiba Prefectural Road Route 107|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617789}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Kenichi Tanikawa|ja|谷川健一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenichi Tanikawa|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2392596}} ''The Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Sites – Volume 11: Kanto'' {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusuisha|1=Hakusuisha Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346470}}, 1984. ISBN 4-560-02221-6 == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Kozaki Manor|ja|神崎荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kozaki Manor|1=Q121862999|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121862999}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kozaki.chiba.jp/05kanko/history.html Kozaki Town History and Cultural Properties] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/7528/ Chiba Tourism Complete Guide: Great Kozaki Kusunoki (Kozaki Town)] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|神崎神社|version=98849858|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:神崎神社]] {{Commons category|Kōzaki-jinja (Kōzaki, Chiba)}} {{wikidata link|Q11589654}} [[Category:Architecture of Kōzaki Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kōzaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kōzaki-jinja (Kōzaki, Chiba)]][[ja:神崎神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kōzaki-jinja (Kōzaki, Chiba)]][[ja:神崎神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Kōzaki-jinja (Kōzaki, Chiba)]][[ja:神崎神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Kozaki-shrine and NanjyaMonjya,Kozaki-town,Japan.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Kōzaki|WD=Q1153721|1=Kōzaki}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.905556|N|140.4|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}}} {{ill|Kōzaki Shrine|ja|神崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzaki Shrine|qq=}} {{Otheruses|Kōzaki Shrine in Chiba Prefecture|Shrine in Kotoura, Tottori Prefecture|Kōzaki Shrine (Kotoura)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |名称= Kōzaki Shrine |image=[[File:2024-kasai_Kozaki_Shrine_and_Kozaki_no_O-kusu_Katori-JPN.jpg|250px|{{ill|Kōzaki Shrine|ja|神崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzaki Shrine|qq=}}
Worship Hall and the Nanjamonja tree]] |所在地= {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|Katori District|en|Katori District|WD=Q780284|lt=Katori District|1=Katori District}}{{ill|Kōzaki|en|Kōzaki|WD=Q1153721|lt=Kozaki Town|1=Kōzaki}} Kozaki Honjuku 1994 |位置= {{coord|35|54|20|N|140|24|0|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja|天鳥船命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥之石楠船神|1=Ame-no-torifune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10731425}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi no Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona no Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} |社格= Prefectural shrine |創建= |本殿= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= Komatsu Shrine |例祭= |神事= }} '''Kōzaki Shrine''' (神崎神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|Katori District|en|Katori District|WD=Q780284|lt=Katori District|1=Katori District}}{{ill|Kōzaki|en|Kōzaki|WD=Q1153721|lt=Kozaki Town|1=Kōzaki}} ({{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province|1=Shimōsa Province}}{{ill|Katori District|en|Katori District|WD=Q780284|lt=Katori District|1=Katori District}}). Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}} was defined under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Prefectural shrine|lt_ja=県社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It enshrines as its main deities {{ill|Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja|天鳥船命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥之石楠船神|1=Ame-no-torifune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10731425}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi no Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}, and {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona no Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}. == Overview == The founding date of this shrine is unknown, but according to its tradition, it is said that in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} era ({{ill|673|en|673|WD=Q31105|lt=673|1=673}}) it was relocated to this site from the two mounds at Ōura Pond, located on the border between {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|WD=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi|1=Hitachi Province}} and Shimōsa. Until the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} period, it was known as "Komatsu Shrine", but in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Genkei|1=Gangyō}} era ({{ill|879|en|879|WD=Q25001|lt=879|1=879}}) it was conferred the lower Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}下) in the {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|en|Shinkai (divine rank)|WD=Q11591025|lt=Shinto Ranking system|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}.In the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', in the entry for April 5 in the 3rd year of the Genkei era, it is recorded: "To the {{ill|Mihosu Shrine|ja|御穂神社|lt=Miryōjin|lt_ja=御廊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosu Shrine|1=Miho Shrine (Q11489249)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489249}} of Suruga Province with the upper Junior Fifth Rank, the lower Senior Fifth Rank is conferred, and to the Komatsu Shrine of Shimōsa Province with the upper Sixth Rank, the lower Junior Fifth Rank is conferred." This shrine is not an auxiliary shrine ({{ill|Auxiliary shrine|ja|末社|lt=Massha|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Auxiliary shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}}) of {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Shrine|1=Katori Jingū}}, but during the periodic rebuilding it played a regular role in constructing the "Asame Hall". Furthermore, according to the ''{{ill|Tone River Illustrated History|ja|利根川図志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tone River Illustrated History|1=Tonegawa Zushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17229342}}'', this shrine was once part of the former territory of a chief priestly family, and its enshrined deity, {{ill|Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja|天鳥船命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥之石楠船神|1=Ame-no-torifune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10731425}}, is regarded as the messenger dispatched under the command of {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} when {{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|lt=Keizutsushi no Kami|1=Futsunushi}} of {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Shrine|1=Katori Jingū}} and {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi no Kami|1=Kashima Jingū}} of {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|lt=Kashima Shrine|1=Kashima Jingū}} negotiated the {{ill|Land Concession|ja|葦原中国平定|lt=transfer of sovereignty|lt_ja=国譲り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Land Concession|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国譲り|1=Kuni-yuzuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621119}} with Ōkuninushi. It is also noted that the Tennichibune, a ship upon which deities ride ({{ill|Ship|ja|諸手船|lt=Various-hand ship|lt_ja=船|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ship|1=Various-hand ship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632096}}), is alternatively known as Tori no Ishikusu-fune no Kami, while this shrine possesses a large {{ill|Camphora officinarum|en|Camphora officinarum|WD=Q158722|lt=Kusunoki|1=Camphora officinarum}}, commonly called the {{ill|Nanjamonja|ja|ナンジャモンジャ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanjamonja|1=Q11324098|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324098}} tree.{{ill|Mito Kōmon|ja|水戸黄門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mito Kōmon|1=stories of Mito Kōmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11129919}} is said to have once pondered, "What on earth is the name of this tree?" Although he did not actually travel throughout Japan, he is known to have visited this shrine. The main tree is surrounded by five saplings; its trunk has a circumference of 13.1 meters and a height of 19 meters, and it is designated as a national {{ill|Natural monument|en|Natural monument|WD=Q23790|lt=Natural Monument|1=Natural monument}} under the name {{ill|Great Kozaki Kusunoki|ja|神崎の大クス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kozaki Kusunoki|1=Kozaki no Okusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124285355}}. The shrine grove, known as the "Kozaki Forest", is designated as a natural monument of Chiba Prefecture ({{ill|Natural Monuments of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#天然記念物|lt=Chiba Prefecture Natural Monuments|lt_ja=千葉県の天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural Monuments of Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture Natural Monuments|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}). [[File:Kōzaki-jinja_(Kōzaki,_Chiba)_12.jpg|thumb|right|230px|{{ill|Great Kozaki Kusunoki|ja|神崎の大クス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kozaki Kusunoki|1=Kozaki no Okusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124285355}}. Commonly known as the Nanjamonja tree.]] The shrine has long received strong patronage from successive rulers, and it is said that during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|Sengoku period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} it possessed shrine domains covering 700 {{ill|Town (unit)|ja|町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Town (unit)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Towns of Japan|WD=Q1059478}} ({{ill|Jisha-ryō|en|Jisha-ryō|WD=Q11458058|lt=Shrine domain|1=Jisha-ryō}}). During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it held 20 koku of {{ill|Shuin land|ja|朱印地・黒印地|lt=Red Seal Land|lt_ja=朱印領|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin land|1=shuinchi and kokuinchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521520}}, and the "Kōzaki Shrine Documents"—a collection of ancient manuscripts and illustrations passed down from the early {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}—is considered an extremely important historical source of the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|WD=Q12554|lt=Medieval|1=Middle Ages}} period ({{ill|Historical source|en|Historical source|WD=Q3750478|lt=Historical source|1=Historical source}}), having been designated as a tangible cultural property (ancient documents) of Chiba Prefecture ({{ill|Tangible Cultural Property (Ancient Documents) of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#古文書|lt=Chiba Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property (Ancient Documents)|lt_ja=千葉県の有形文化財（古文書）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tangible Cultural Property (Ancient Documents) of Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property (Ancient Documents)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}). Furthermore, the "Jinguji Documents" held by the former head temple ({{ill|Head temple|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Bettō-ji|WD=Q11396755}}) {{ill|Jinguji|ja|神宮寺 (千葉県神崎町)|lt=Jinguji|lt_ja=神宮寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinguji|1=Jingū-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589479}}, despite missing 60 out of 600 volumes of the {{ill|Large Prajñāpāramitā Sūtras|en|Large Prajñāpāramitā Sūtras|WD=Q15905785|lt=Mahaprajnaparamita Sutra|1=Large Prajñāpāramitā Sūtras}}, are highly valued as a sutra transcription from the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}. The colophon ({{ill|Colophon|ja|奥書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Colophon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥付|1=Colophon (publishing)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q372474}}) reveals details about the temples ({{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|WD=Q5393308|lt=Temple|1=Buddhist temple}}), monks ({{ill|Monk|en|Monk|WD=Q733786|lt=Monk|1=Monk}}), and the sphere of religious influence at that time, and it has also been designated as a tangible cultural property of Chiba Prefecture ({{ill|Tangible Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#古文書|lt=Chiba Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property|lt_ja=千葉県の有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tangible Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}). == Transportation == * About a 20‐minute walk from {{ill|Narita Line|en|Narita Line|WD=Q1070027|lt=Narita Line|1=Narita Line}}{{ill|Shimōsa-Kōzaki Station|en|Shimōsa-Kōzaki Station|WD=Q7497064|lt=Shimōsa-Kozaki Station|1=Shimōsa-Kōzaki Station}} * Near the south end of Kozaki Ōhashi on {{ill|Japan National Route 356|en|Japan National Route 356|WD=Q6157325|lt=National Route 356|1=Japan National Route 356}} and {{ill|Ibaraki and Chiba Prefectural Road 107, Edosaki-Kozaki Line|ja|茨城県道・千葉県道107号江戸崎神崎線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki and Chiba Prefectural Road 107, Edosaki-Kozaki Line|1=Ibaraki Prefectural Road and Chiba Prefectural Road Route 107|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617789}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Kenichi Tanikawa|ja|谷川健一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenichi Tanikawa|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2392596}} ''The Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Sites – Volume 11: Kanto'' {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusuisha|1=Hakusuisha Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346470}}, 1984. ISBN 4-560-02221-6 == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Kozaki Manor|ja|神崎荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kozaki Manor|1=Q121862999|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121862999}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kozaki.chiba.jp/05kanko/history.html Kozaki Town History and Cultural Properties] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/7528/ Chiba Tourism Complete Guide: Great Kozaki Kusunoki (Kozaki Town)] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|神崎神社|version=98849858|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:神崎神社]] {{Commons category|Kōzaki-jinja (Kōzaki, Chiba)}} {{wikidata link|Q11589654}} [[Category:Architecture of Kōzaki Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kōzaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kōzaki-jinja (Kōzaki, Chiba)]][[ja:神崎神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kōzaki-jinja (Kōzaki, Chiba)]][[ja:神崎神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Kōzaki-jinja (Kōzaki, Chiba)]][[ja:神崎神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Kozaki-shrine and NanjyaMonjya,Kozaki-town,Japan.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Kōzaki|WD=Q1153721|1=Kōzaki}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.905556|N|140.4|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}}} {{ill|Kōzaki Shrine|ja|神崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzaki Shrine|qq=}} {{Otheruses|Kōzaki Shrine in Chiba Prefecture|Shrine in Kotoura, Tottori Prefecture|Kōzaki Shrine (Kotoura)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |名称= Kōzaki Shrine |image=[[File:2024-kasai_Kozaki_Shrine_and_Kozaki_no_O-kusu_Katori-JPN.jpg|250px|{{ill|Kōzaki Shrine|ja|神崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzaki Shrine|qq=}}
Worship Hall and the Nanjamonja tree]] |所在地= {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|Katori District|en|Katori District|WD=Q780284|lt=Katori District|1=Katori District}}{{ill|Kōzaki|en|Kōzaki|WD=Q1153721|lt=Kozaki Town|1=Kōzaki}} Kozaki Honjuku 1994 |位置= {{coord|35|54|20|N|140|24|0|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja|天鳥船命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥之石楠船神|1=Ame-no-torifune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10731425}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi no Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona no Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} |社格= Prefectural shrine |創建= |本殿= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= Komatsu Shrine |例祭= |神事= }} '''Kōzaki Shrine''' (神崎神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|Katori District|en|Katori District|WD=Q780284|lt=Katori District|1=Katori District}}{{ill|Kōzaki|en|Kōzaki|WD=Q1153721|lt=Kozaki Town|1=Kōzaki}} ({{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province|1=Shimōsa Province}}{{ill|Katori District|en|Katori District|WD=Q780284|lt=Katori District|1=Katori District}}). Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}} was defined under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Prefectural shrine|lt_ja=県社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It enshrines as its main deities {{ill|Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja|天鳥船命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥之石楠船神|1=Ame-no-torifune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10731425}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi no Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}, and {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona no Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}. == Overview == The founding date of this shrine is unknown, but according to its tradition, it is said that in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} era ({{ill|673|en|673|WD=Q31105|lt=673|1=673}}) it was relocated to this site from the two mounds at Ōura Pond, located on the border between {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|WD=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi|1=Hitachi Province}} and Shimōsa. Until the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} period, it was known as "Komatsu Shrine", but in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Genkei|1=Gangyō}} era ({{ill|879|en|879|WD=Q25001|lt=879|1=879}}) it was conferred the lower Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}下) in the {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|en|Shinkai (divine rank)|WD=Q11591025|lt=Shinto Ranking system|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}.In the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', in the entry for April 5 in the 3rd year of the Genkei era, it is recorded: "To the {{ill|Mihosu Shrine|ja|御穂神社|lt=Miryōjin|lt_ja=御廊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosu Shrine|1=Miho Shrine (Q11489249)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489249}} of Suruga Province with the upper Junior Fifth Rank, the lower Senior Fifth Rank is conferred, and to the Komatsu Shrine of Shimōsa Province with the upper Sixth Rank, the lower Junior Fifth Rank is conferred." This shrine is not an auxiliary shrine ({{ill|Auxiliary shrine|ja|末社|lt=Massha|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Auxiliary shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}}) of {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Shrine|1=Katori Jingū}}, but during the periodic rebuilding it played a regular role in constructing the "Asame Hall". Furthermore, according to the ''{{ill|Tone River Illustrated History|ja|利根川図志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tone River Illustrated History|1=Tonegawa Zushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17229342}}'', this shrine was once part of the former territory of a chief priestly family, and its enshrined deity, {{ill|Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja|天鳥船命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥之石楠船神|1=Ame-no-torifune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10731425}}, is regarded as the messenger dispatched under the command of {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} when {{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|lt=Keizutsushi no Kami|1=Futsunushi}} of {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Shrine|1=Katori Jingū}} and {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi no Kami|1=Kashima Jingū}} of {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|lt=Kashima Shrine|1=Kashima Jingū}} negotiated the {{ill|Land Concession|ja|葦原中国平定|lt=transfer of sovereignty|lt_ja=国譲り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Land Concession|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国譲り|1=Kuni-yuzuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621119}} with Ōkuninushi. It is also noted that the Tennichibune, a ship upon which deities ride ({{ill|Ship|ja|諸手船|lt=Various-hand ship|lt_ja=船|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ship|1=Various-hand ship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632096}}), is alternatively known as Tori no Ishikusu-fune no Kami, while this shrine possesses a large {{ill|Camphora officinarum|en|Camphora officinarum|WD=Q158722|lt=Kusunoki|1=Camphora officinarum}}, commonly called the {{ill|Nanjamonja|ja|ナンジャモンジャ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanjamonja|1=Q11324098|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324098}} tree.{{ill|Mito Kōmon|ja|水戸黄門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mito Kōmon|1=stories of Mito Kōmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11129919}} is said to have once pondered, "What on earth is the name of this tree?" Although he did not actually travel throughout Japan, he is known to have visited this shrine. The main tree is surrounded by five saplings; its trunk has a circumference of 13.1 meters and a height of 19 meters, and it is designated as a national {{ill|Natural monument|en|Natural monument|WD=Q23790|lt=Natural Monument|1=Natural monument}} under the name {{ill|Great Kozaki Kusunoki|ja|神崎の大クス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kozaki Kusunoki|1=Kozaki no Okusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124285355}}. The shrine grove, known as the "Kozaki Forest", is designated as a natural monument of Chiba Prefecture ({{ill|Natural Monuments of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#天然記念物|lt=Chiba Prefecture Natural Monuments|lt_ja=千葉県の天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural Monuments of Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture Natural Monuments|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}). [[File:Kōzaki-jinja_(Kōzaki,_Chiba)_12.jpg|thumb|right|230px|{{ill|Great Kozaki Kusunoki|ja|神崎の大クス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kozaki Kusunoki|1=Kozaki no Okusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124285355}}. Commonly known as the Nanjamonja tree.]] The shrine has long received strong patronage from successive rulers, and it is said that during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|Sengoku period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} it possessed shrine domains covering 700 {{ill|Town (unit)|ja|町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Town (unit)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Towns of Japan|WD=Q1059478}} ({{ill|Jisha-ryō|en|Jisha-ryō|WD=Q11458058|lt=Shrine domain|1=Jisha-ryō}}). During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it held 20 koku of {{ill|Shuin land|ja|朱印地・黒印地|lt=Red Seal Land|lt_ja=朱印領|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin land|1=shuinchi and kokuinchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521520}}, and the "Kōzaki Shrine Documents"—a collection of ancient manuscripts and illustrations passed down from the early {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}—is considered an extremely important historical source of the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|WD=Q12554|lt=Medieval|1=Middle Ages}} period ({{ill|Historical source|en|Historical source|WD=Q3750478|lt=Historical source|1=Historical source}}), having been designated as a tangible cultural property (ancient documents) of Chiba Prefecture ({{ill|Tangible Cultural Property (Ancient Documents) of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#古文書|lt=Chiba Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property (Ancient Documents)|lt_ja=千葉県の有形文化財（古文書）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tangible Cultural Property (Ancient Documents) of Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property (Ancient Documents)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}). Furthermore, the "Jinguji Documents" held by the former head temple ({{ill|Head temple|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Bettō-ji|WD=Q11396755}}) {{ill|Jinguji|ja|神宮寺 (千葉県神崎町)|lt=Jinguji|lt_ja=神宮寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinguji|1=Jingū-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589479}}, despite missing 60 out of 600 volumes of the {{ill|Large Prajñāpāramitā Sūtras|en|Large Prajñāpāramitā Sūtras|WD=Q15905785|lt=Mahaprajnaparamita Sutra|1=Large Prajñāpāramitā Sūtras}}, are highly valued as a sutra transcription from the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}. The colophon ({{ill|Colophon|ja|奥書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Colophon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥付|1=Colophon (publishing)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q372474}}) reveals details about the temples ({{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|WD=Q5393308|lt=Temple|1=Buddhist temple}}), monks ({{ill|Monk|en|Monk|WD=Q733786|lt=Monk|1=Monk}}), and the sphere of religious influence at that time, and it has also been designated as a tangible cultural property of Chiba Prefecture ({{ill|Tangible Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#古文書|lt=Chiba Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property|lt_ja=千葉県の有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tangible Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}). == Transportation == * About a 20‐minute walk from {{ill|Narita Line|en|Narita Line|WD=Q1070027|lt=Narita Line|1=Narita Line}}{{ill|Shimōsa-Kōzaki Station|en|Shimōsa-Kōzaki Station|WD=Q7497064|lt=Shimōsa-Kozaki Station|1=Shimōsa-Kōzaki Station}} * Near the south end of Kozaki Ōhashi on {{ill|Japan National Route 356|en|Japan National Route 356|WD=Q6157325|lt=National Route 356|1=Japan National Route 356}} and {{ill|Ibaraki and Chiba Prefectural Road 107, Edosaki-Kozaki Line|ja|茨城県道・千葉県道107号江戸崎神崎線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki and Chiba Prefectural Road 107, Edosaki-Kozaki Line|1=Ibaraki Prefectural Road and Chiba Prefectural Road Route 107|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617789}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Kenichi Tanikawa|ja|谷川健一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenichi Tanikawa|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2392596}} ''The Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Sites – Volume 11: Kanto'' {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusuisha|1=Hakusuisha Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346470}}, 1984. ISBN 4-560-02221-6 == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Kozaki Manor|ja|神崎荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kozaki Manor|1=Q121862999|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121862999}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kozaki.chiba.jp/05kanko/history.html Kozaki Town History and Cultural Properties] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/7528/ Chiba Tourism Complete Guide: Great Kozaki Kusunoki (Kozaki Town)] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|神崎神社|version=98849858|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:神崎神社]] {{Commons category|Kōzaki-jinja (Kōzaki, Chiba)}} {{wikidata link|Q11589654}} [[Category:Architecture of Kōzaki Town]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kōzaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Kōzaki-jinja (Kōzaki, Chiba)]][[ja:神崎神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Kōzaki-jinja (Kōzaki, Chiba)]][[ja:神崎神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Kōzaki-jinja (Kōzaki, Chiba)]][[ja:神崎神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Kozaki-shrine and NanjyaMonjya,Kozaki-town,Japan.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Kōzaki|WD=Q1153721|1=Kōzaki}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.905556|N|140.4|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}}} {{ill|Kōzaki Shrine|ja|神崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzaki Shrine|qq=}} {{Otheruses|Kōzaki Shrine in Chiba Prefecture|Shrine in Kotoura, Tottori Prefecture|Kōzaki Shrine (Kotoura)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |名称= Kōzaki Shrine |image=[[File:2024-kasai_Kozaki_Shrine_and_Kozaki_no_O-kusu_Katori-JPN.jpg|250px|{{ill|Kōzaki Shrine|ja|神崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzaki Shrine|qq=}}
Worship Hall and the Nanjamonja tree]] |所在地= {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|Katori District|en|Katori District|WD=Q780284|lt=Katori District|1=Katori District}}{{ill|Kōzaki|en|Kōzaki|WD=Q1153721|lt=Kozaki Town|1=Kōzaki}} Kozaki Honjuku 1994 |位置= {{coord|35|54|20|N|140|24|0|E|region:JP-12_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja|天鳥船命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥之石楠船神|1=Ame-no-torifune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10731425}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi no Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona no Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}} |社格= Prefectural shrine |創建= |本殿= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名= Komatsu Shrine |例祭= |神事= }} '''Kōzaki Shrine''' (神崎神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}{{ill|Katori District|en|Katori District|WD=Q780284|lt=Katori District|1=Katori District}}{{ill|Kōzaki|en|Kōzaki|WD=Q1153721|lt=Kozaki Town|1=Kōzaki}} ({{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province|1=Shimōsa Province}}{{ill|Katori District|en|Katori District|WD=Q780284|lt=Katori District|1=Katori District}}). Its former {{ill|Shrine Rank|ja|社格|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shakaku|WD=Q10444029}} was defined under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|近代社格制度#諸社|lt=Prefectural shrine|lt_ja=県社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It enshrines as its main deities {{ill|Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja|天鳥船命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥之石楠船神|1=Ame-no-torifune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10731425}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi no Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}, and {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|lt=Sukunahikona no Mikoto|1=Sukunabikona}}. == Overview == The founding date of this shrine is unknown, but according to its tradition, it is said that in the 2nd year of the {{ill|Hakuhō|ja|白鳳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuhō|1=Hakuhō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580612}} era ({{ill|673|en|673|WD=Q31105|lt=673|1=673}}) it was relocated to this site from the two mounds at Ōura Pond, located on the border between {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|WD=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi|1=Hitachi Province}} and Shimōsa. Until the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} period, it was known as "Komatsu Shrine", but in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Genkei|1=Gangyō}} era ({{ill|879|en|879|WD=Q25001|lt=879|1=879}}) it was conferred the lower Junior Fifth Rank ({{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}下) in the {{ill|Shinkai (divine rank)|en|Shinkai (divine rank)|WD=Q11591025|lt=Shinto Ranking system|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}}.In the ''{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'', in the entry for April 5 in the 3rd year of the Genkei era, it is recorded: "To the {{ill|Mihosu Shrine|ja|御穂神社|lt=Miryōjin|lt_ja=御廊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihosu Shrine|1=Miho Shrine (Q11489249)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489249}} of Suruga Province with the upper Junior Fifth Rank, the lower Senior Fifth Rank is conferred, and to the Komatsu Shrine of Shimōsa Province with the upper Sixth Rank, the lower Junior Fifth Rank is conferred." This shrine is not an auxiliary shrine ({{ill|Auxiliary shrine|ja|末社|lt=Massha|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Auxiliary shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 摂末社}}) of {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Shrine|1=Katori Jingū}}, but during the periodic rebuilding it played a regular role in constructing the "Asame Hall". Furthermore, according to the ''{{ill|Tone River Illustrated History|ja|利根川図志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tone River Illustrated History|1=Tonegawa Zushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17229342}}'', this shrine was once part of the former territory of a chief priestly family, and its enshrined deity, {{ill|Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja|天鳥船命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennichibune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥之石楠船神|1=Ame-no-torifune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10731425}}, is regarded as the messenger dispatched under the command of {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} when {{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|lt=Keizutsushi no Kami|1=Futsunushi}} of {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Shrine|1=Katori Jingū}} and {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi no Kami|1=Kashima Jingū}} of {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|lt=Kashima Shrine|1=Kashima Jingū}} negotiated the {{ill|Land Concession|ja|葦原中国平定|lt=transfer of sovereignty|lt_ja=国譲り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Land Concession|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国譲り|1=Kuni-yuzuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621119}} with Ōkuninushi. It is also noted that the Tennichibune, a ship upon which deities ride ({{ill|Ship|ja|諸手船|lt=Various-hand ship|lt_ja=船|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ship|1=Various-hand ship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632096}}), is alternatively known as Tori no Ishikusu-fune no Kami, while this shrine possesses a large {{ill|Camphora officinarum|en|Camphora officinarum|WD=Q158722|lt=Kusunoki|1=Camphora officinarum}}, commonly called the {{ill|Nanjamonja|ja|ナンジャモンジャ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanjamonja|1=Q11324098|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11324098}} tree.{{ill|Mito Kōmon|ja|水戸黄門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mito Kōmon|1=stories of Mito Kōmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11129919}} is said to have once pondered, "What on earth is the name of this tree?" Although he did not actually travel throughout Japan, he is known to have visited this shrine. The main tree is surrounded by five saplings; its trunk has a circumference of 13.1 meters and a height of 19 meters, and it is designated as a national {{ill|Natural monument|en|Natural monument|WD=Q23790|lt=Natural Monument|1=Natural monument}} under the name {{ill|Great Kozaki Kusunoki|ja|神崎の大クス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kozaki Kusunoki|1=Kozaki no Okusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124285355}}. The shrine grove, known as the "Kozaki Forest", is designated as a natural monument of Chiba Prefecture ({{ill|Natural Monuments of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#天然記念物|lt=Chiba Prefecture Natural Monuments|lt_ja=千葉県の天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural Monuments of Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture Natural Monuments|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}). [[File:Kōzaki-jinja_(Kōzaki,_Chiba)_12.jpg|thumb|right|230px|{{ill|Great Kozaki Kusunoki|ja|神崎の大クス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kozaki Kusunoki|1=Kozaki no Okusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q124285355}}. Commonly known as the Nanjamonja tree.]] The shrine has long received strong patronage from successive rulers, and it is said that during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|Sengoku period|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} it possessed shrine domains covering 700 {{ill|Town (unit)|ja|町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Town (unit)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Towns of Japan|WD=Q1059478}} ({{ill|Jisha-ryō|en|Jisha-ryō|WD=Q11458058|lt=Shrine domain|1=Jisha-ryō}}). During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it held 20 koku of {{ill|Shuin land|ja|朱印地・黒印地|lt=Red Seal Land|lt_ja=朱印領|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuin land|1=shuinchi and kokuinchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11521520}}, and the "Kōzaki Shrine Documents"—a collection of ancient manuscripts and illustrations passed down from the early {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}—is considered an extremely important historical source of the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|WD=Q12554|lt=Medieval|1=Middle Ages}} period ({{ill|Historical source|en|Historical source|WD=Q3750478|lt=Historical source|1=Historical source}}), having been designated as a tangible cultural property (ancient documents) of Chiba Prefecture ({{ill|Tangible Cultural Property (Ancient Documents) of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#古文書|lt=Chiba Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property (Ancient Documents)|lt_ja=千葉県の有形文化財（古文書）|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tangible Cultural Property (Ancient Documents) of Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property (Ancient Documents)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}). Furthermore, the "Jinguji Documents" held by the former head temple ({{ill|Head temple|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Head temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Bettō-ji|WD=Q11396755}}) {{ill|Jinguji|ja|神宮寺 (千葉県神崎町)|lt=Jinguji|lt_ja=神宮寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinguji|1=Jingū-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589479}}, despite missing 60 out of 600 volumes of the {{ill|Large Prajñāpāramitā Sūtras|en|Large Prajñāpāramitā Sūtras|WD=Q15905785|lt=Mahaprajnaparamita Sutra|1=Large Prajñāpāramitā Sūtras}}, are highly valued as a sutra transcription from the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}. The colophon ({{ill|Colophon|ja|奥書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Colophon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 奥付|1=Colophon (publishing)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q372474}}) reveals details about the temples ({{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|WD=Q5393308|lt=Temple|1=Buddhist temple}}), monks ({{ill|Monk|en|Monk|WD=Q733786|lt=Monk|1=Monk}}), and the sphere of religious influence at that time, and it has also been designated as a tangible cultural property of Chiba Prefecture ({{ill|Tangible Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県指定文化財一覧#古文書|lt=Chiba Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property|lt_ja=千葉県の有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tangible Cultural Property of Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406175}}). == Transportation == * About a 20‐minute walk from {{ill|Narita Line|en|Narita Line|WD=Q1070027|lt=Narita Line|1=Narita Line}}{{ill|Shimōsa-Kōzaki Station|en|Shimōsa-Kōzaki Station|WD=Q7497064|lt=Shimōsa-Kozaki Station|1=Shimōsa-Kōzaki Station}} * Near the south end of Kozaki Ōhashi on {{ill|Japan National Route 356|en|Japan National Route 356|WD=Q6157325|lt=National Route 356|1=Japan National Route 356}} and {{ill|Ibaraki and Chiba Prefectural Road 107, Edosaki-Kozaki Line|ja|茨城県道・千葉県道107号江戸崎神崎線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki and Chiba Prefectural Road 107, Edosaki-Kozaki Line|1=Ibaraki Prefectural Road and Chiba Prefectural Road Route 107|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617789}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{ill|Kenichi Tanikawa|ja|谷川健一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenichi Tanikawa|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2392596}} ''The Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Sites – Volume 11: Kanto'' {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusuisha|1=Hakusuisha Publishing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346470}}, 1984. ISBN 4-560-02221-6 == See Also == {{Commonscat}} * {{ill|Kozaki Manor|ja|神崎荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kozaki Manor|1=Q121862999|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q121862999}} == External Links == * [http://www.town.kozaki.chiba.jp/05kanko/history.html Kozaki Town History and Cultural Properties] * [http://maruchiba.jp/sys/data/index/page/id/7528/ Chiba Tourism Complete Guide: Great Kozaki Kusunoki (Kozaki Town)] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|神崎神社|version=98849858|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:神崎神社]] {{Commons category|Kōzaki-jinja (Kōzaki, Chiba)}} {{wikidata link|Q11589654}} [[Category:Architecture of Kōzaki Town]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kōzaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
